category_title: Students who exhibit frustration through crying and self-abusive behavior during long tasks
student_description: Shaun is an 11-year-old student who exhibits severe distress, including crying, garbled vocalizations, and self-abusive behavior, when overwhelmed by specific environmental factors. The evidence reveals he uniquely requires extreme task segmentation (one page at a time) and very close physical proximity (within 0.3 meters) from staff to maintain engagement and prevent behavioral escalation.

criteria.rationale: Shaun is easily frustrated by the perceived volume of tasks (like stapled packets), which triggers severe distress and self-abusive behavior.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly presents only one small portion of the task at a time (e.g., one question or one single page) and avoids referencing or showing the entire packet or total workload.

criteria.rationale: Shaun requires staff to be within 0.3 meters during instructions to maintain engagement and prevent undesirable behaviors.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly states or implies they are moving very close to Shaun (e.g., sitting right next to him, moving within arm's reach) before or while giving instructions.
